content: read reviews/1024.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/1024.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/1024.md.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/1024.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19‚Äď36).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 1024`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1024`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (minimum independence number of 3-uniform linear hypergraphs, i.e. partial Steiner triple systems).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[PhRo86]` Phelps & R√∂dl (1986).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1024 ... end Erdos1024` (lines 40, 87).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1024`.
- [x] `open Finset` appears before the namespace block (line 38).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `Hypergraph3Uniform` (structure, line 43) has a LaTeX docstring (line 42).
- [x] `Hypergraph3Uniform.IsLinear` (def, line 49) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 47‚Äď48).
- [x] `Hypergraph3Uniform.IsIndependent` (def, line 54) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 52‚Äď53).
- [x] `Hypergraph3Uniform.independenceNumber` (def, line 60) has a LaTeX docstring (line 58) and is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sSup`).
- [x] `linearHypergraphIndNum` (def, line 66) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 63‚Äď65) and is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `Hypergraph3Uniform`, `IsLinear`, `IsIndependent`.
- [x] Functions returning values use `lowerCamelCase`: `independenceNumber`, `linearHypergraphIndNum`.
- N/A: No custom notation used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks added (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword: `theorem erdos_1024`.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1024` (matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows `snake_case` convention for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 79).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 70‚Äď78) describing the Phelps‚ÄďR√∂dl result f(n) ‚Čć (n log n)^{1/2}.
- [x] Theorem docstring references source paper: `[PhRo86]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is stated as a direct assertion about asymptotic bounds on f(n). The solved result is expressed as the existence of constants C‚āĀ, C‚āā bounding f(n) above and below. **Case 3** applies ‚ÄĒ direct assertion, no `answer()` wrapper needed.

- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly ‚ÄĒ no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for a direct assertion of asymptotic bounds.
- [x] All quantifiers (`‚ąÉ C‚āĀ C‚āā`, `‚ąÉ N‚āÄ`, `‚ąÄ n`) are in the theorem statement, not as parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]`: `category research solved`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` is in the allowed list.
- [x] `research solved` theorem contains `sorry` (proof not yet formally verified ‚ÄĒ expected and acceptable).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (one: 5).
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order (trivially ‚ÄĒ one entry).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] 5 (Combinatorics) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code. Appropriate for a hypergraph independence problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by sorry` form (`:= by\n  sorry` on lines 84‚Äď85, multi-line form is acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single statement about asymptotic bounds, so this is appropriate.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąą`, `‚Č†`, `‚ą©`, `‚Č§`, `¬¨`, `‚äÜ`, `‚ÜĎ`, `‚ąß`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest line ~80 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified ‚ÄĒ requires `lake build` execution.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified.

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS (modulo build verification)**

The file is clean and conforms to all checklist requirements. The formalization faithfully captures the Phelps‚ÄďR√∂dl result: the minimum independence number f(n) over all 3-uniform linear hypergraphs on n vertices satisfies C‚āĀ‚ąö(n log n) ‚Č§ f(n) ‚Č§ C‚āā‚ąö(n log n). The five helper definitions (`Hypergraph3Uniform`, `IsLinear`, `IsIndependent`, `independenceNumber`, `linearHypergraphIndNum`) are all documented with LaTeX docstrings, correctly cased, and marked `noncomputable` where appropriate (the two using `sSup`/`sInf`). The `answer()` usage is correct (Case 3 ‚ÄĒ direct assertion of asymptotic bounds, no wrapper needed). AMS code 5 (Combinatorics) is appropriate for this hypergraph problem. The theorem docstring and module docstring both cite `[PhRo86]`.

content.text: The review status is **PASS** (modulo build verification). No changes needed.
